name: Cochrane Risk of Bias 2 (RoB 2)
overall_judgment_rule: Low only if every domain is Low. High if any domain is High. 'No information' for a domain is treated as Some concerns when computing the overall rating. Otherwise Some concerns.

domains.name: Bias arising from the randomization process
retrieval_queries: randomization sequence generation method computer random number
allocation concealment sealed envelopes central allocation
signaling_questions: Was the allocation sequence concealed until participants were enrolled and assigned to interventions?
Did baseline differences between intervention groups suggest a problem with the randomization process?
domains.criteria_summary: Low: random sequence generated AND allocation concealed AND no concerning baseline imbalance. Some concerns: information missing on one or more items but no evidence of a problem. High: non-random sequence, broken allocation concealment, or baseline imbalances suggesting subverted randomization.

domains.name: Bias due to deviations from intended interventions
retrieval_queries: intention to treat per protocol analysis
signaling_questions: Were participants aware of their assigned intervention during the trial?
Were carers and people delivering the interventions aware of participants' assigned intervention?
Were there deviations from the intended intervention that arose because of the trial context?
Was an appropriate analysis used to estimate the effect of assignment to intervention?
domains.criteria_summary: Low: blinded OR no deviations beyond what would happen in usual care, AND appropriate analysis (e.g., ITT). Some concerns: information unclear or possible minor deviations. High: known unblinded deviations affecting the outcome, or inappropriate analysis.

domains.name: Bias due to missing outcome data
retrieval_queries: loss to follow up missing outcome data attrition
signaling_questions: Were data for this outcome available for all, or nearly all, participants randomized?
Is there evidence that the result was not biased by missing outcome data?
Could missingness in the outcome depend on its true value?
domains.criteria_summary: Low: outcome data available for nearly all (>=95%) participants OR analysis adequately addresses missingness. Some concerns: moderate missingness with uncertain impact. High: substantial missingness likely related to the outcome value.

domains.name: Bias in measurement of the outcome
signaling_questions: Was the method of measuring the outcome inappropriate?
Could measurement or ascertainment of the outcome have differed between intervention groups?
Were outcome assessors aware of the intervention received by study participants?
Could assessment of the outcome have been influenced by knowledge of intervention received?
domains.criteria_summary: Low: appropriate measurement, same across groups, blinded assessors OR objective outcome unaffected by knowledge of group. Some concerns: assessor blinding unclear for subjective outcomes. High: assessor unblinded for subjective outcome, or differential measurement between groups.

domains.name: Bias in selection of the reported result
signaling_questions: Were the data analysed in accordance with a pre-specified analysis plan finalised before unblinded outcome data were available?
Is the reported outcome likely to have been selected from multiple measurements within the outcome domain?
Is the reported outcome likely to have been selected from multiple analyses of the data?
domains.criteria_summary: Low: outcome and analysis pre-specified (registered protocol), no evidence of selective reporting. Some concerns: protocol unavailable but no obvious selective reporting. High: clear selective reporting from multiple measurements or analyses.
